chunk_summary: This section discusses national program linkages for adolescent nutrition care in India, focusing on RKSK, WIFS, and PM-POSHAN schemes for anemia prevention, deworming, and school meal access.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of RKSK in adolescent nutrition care and its integration with NHM systems.
Learn the operational details of the Weekly Iron-Folic Acid Supplementation (WIFS) program.
Recognize the importance of school meal schemes like PM-POSHAN in addressing undernutrition.
Identify clinical and programmatic strategies for managing anemia in adolescents.
key_facts: The RKSK framework prioritizes adolescent nutrition and provides referral systems for counseling, screening, and supplementation.
WIFS includes supervised weekly iron-folic acid ingestion and biannual deworming.
Program success depends on verifying enrollment, adherence, and addressing side effects or myths.
PM-POSHAN ensures school meal access, critical for undernourished or migrating adolescents.
Clinical anemia management should align with programmatic prevention and continuation strategies.
Trainees must consult updated health ministry documents for accurate dosage and implementation details.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: The national adolescent health framework (RKSK) includes nutrition as a priority domain and provides a referral backbone for counseling, screening, and linkage to supplements and deworming under NHM systems.
document_key_topics: RKSK and its role in nutrition care
